task.title: Fix InMemoryCache TTL — prevent OOM memory leak
task.why: InMemoryCache.set() accepts ttl_seconds but ignores it entirely. cache.set() calls in model_resolver.py never pass ttl_seconds. After 24h production: cache grows unbounded → OOM kill → downtime. Also: RedisCache falls back silently to InMemoryCache on connect error even when REQUIRE_REDIS_FOR_READY=true. This must be an explicit error.

implementation.step:
Add ttl_seconds to every cache.set() call. Import MODEL_CACHE_TTL.
# Add import at top: from app.services.cache_service import cache, MODEL_CACHE_TTL
# Replace all bare cache.set() calls: # OLD: cache.set(cache_key, {...}) # NEW: cache.set(cache_key, {...}, ttl_seconds=MODEL_CACHE_TTL)
# Three places to patch: # 1. After DB artifact found: cache.set(cache_key, {"artifact": artifact_response.model_dump(), "source": "cache_db"}, ttl_seconds=MODEL_CACHE_TTL)
# 2. After vendor asset found: cache.set(cache_key, {"artifact": artifact_response.model_dump(), "source": "vendor_exact"}, ttl_seconds=MODEL_CACHE_TTL)
# 3. After inline generation: cache.set(cache_key, {"artifact": artifact_response, "source": "generated_parametric"}, ttl_seconds=MODEL_CACHE_TTL)

tests.code:
import time from app.services.cache_service import InMemoryCache, MODEL_CACHE_TTL
def test_in_memory_cache_ttl_expires_entry(): c = InMemoryCache() c.set("k", "v", ttl_seconds=1) assert c.get("k") == "v" time.sleep(1.05) assert c.get("k") is None
def test_in_memory_cache_no_ttl_persists(): c = InMemoryCache() c.set("k", "v", ttl_seconds=None) assert c.get("k") == "v"
def test_in_memory_cache_expired_key_removed(): c = InMemoryCache() c.set("k", "v", ttl_seconds=1) time.sleep(1.05) c.get("k") # trigger eviction assert "k" not in c._data
def test_in_memory_cache_default_ttl_is_24h(): c = InMemoryCache() c.set("k", "v") # no explicit ttl_seconds entry = c._data["k"] # expires_at should be about 86400s from now remaining = entry.expires_at - time.monotonic() assert 86390 < remaining <= 86400
def test_model_cache_ttl_constant_is_24h(): assert MODEL_CACHE_TTL == 86_400

task.commit_message: fix(T02): InMemoryCache TTL — prevent OOM + explicit TTL on all cache.set() calls

task.title: Fix Celery broker — fail-fast, no silent memory fallback
task.why: workers/tasks.py uses `broker=settings.redis_url or "memory://"`. If REDIS_URL is not set, Celery uses in-process memory broker silently. All queued jobs are lost on container restart. No warning is logged. Fix: lazy init that raises loudly if Redis missing when dispatch is called.

step.code:
""" Celery task definitions and dispatch helpers.
Production requirement: REDIS_URL must be set. Local dev without Redis: set MODEL_SYNC_GENERATION=true to avoid dispatch. """ from __future__ import annotations
import logging from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
from app.core.config import settings
if TYPE_CHECKING: from celery import Celery
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
_celery_app: "Celery | None" = None
TASK_ROUTES = { "app.workers.tasks.run_generation_job": {"queue": "preview_fast"}, }
def get_celery_app() -> "Celery": """ Lazy init. Raises RuntimeError if REDIS_URL not configured. This prevents silent job loss from memory:// broker. """ global _celery_app if _celery_app is not None: return _celery_app
if not settings.redis_url: raise RuntimeError( "REDIS_URL is required for Celery workers. " "Set REDIS_URL in environment variables. " "For local dev without Redis, use MODEL_SYNC_GENERATION=true " "to run generation inline (not queued)." )
try: from celery import Celery except ImportError as exc: raise RuntimeError("celery package is required: pip install celery") from exc
app = Celery( "fastener_cad", broker=settings.redis_url, backend=settings.redis_url, ) app.conf.update( task_serializer="json", result_serializer="json", accept_content=["json"], task_acks_late=True, task_reject_on_worker_lost=True, worker_prefetch_multiplier=1, task_routes={ "app.workers.tasks.run_generation_job_task": { "queue": "preview_fast" }, }, broker_connection_retry_on_startup=True, ) _celery_app = app return app
def dispatch_generation_job(queue_name: str, job_id: str) -> bool: """ Dispatch a job to Celery. Returns True on success. If Redis not configured: logs warning and returns False (no raise). Caller decides whether to fall back to inline or return 'queued'. """ if not settings.redis_url: logger.warning( "REDIS_URL not configured — skipping async dispatch of job %s " "on queue %s. Set MODEL_SYNC_GENERATION=true for local dev.", job_id, queue_name, ) return False
try: celery_app = get_celery_app() celery_app.send_task( "app.workers.tasks.run_generation_job_task", args=[job_id], queue=queue_name, ) return True except Exception as exc: logger.error("Failed to dispatch job %s: %s", job_id, exc) return False
# ── Celery task (only bound when celery is running) ───────────────────── try: _app = get_celery_app()
@_app.task(name="app.workers.tasks.run_generation_job_task", bind=True, max_retries=3) def run_generation_job_task(self, job_id: str) -> dict: from app.services.job_runner import run_model_generation_job try: return run_model_generation_job(job_id) except Exception as exc: raise self.retry(exc=exc, countdown=10)
except RuntimeError: # Redis not configured — task not bound. OK for local dev. pass
def reset_celery_for_testing() -> None: """Reset cached celery app — use in test teardown only.""" global _celery_app _celery_app = None

task.commit_message: fix(T03): Celery broker fail-fast — no silent memory:// fallback
